html:
THE VENDETTA GAZETTE

Special Edition - March 22, 2026 - "All The News That's Fit To Bootleg"

CAPRICE AND WU-TANG CARVE UP THE CITY IN BLOODY TERRITORIAL WAR

The streets of Vendetta City ran red with ambition this week as two ruthless kingpins launched the most audacious land grab seen since the Great Turf War of '25! Sir Caprice, the dapper don of the downtown district, muscled his way to an astounding 98 new territories, while Wu-Tang, the Iron Fist of the East Side, wasn't far behind with a bone-crushing 90 turfs added to his empire.

Sources close to the action report that Sir Caprice's boys have been moving like greased lightning through the city's prime real estate, their Tommy guns chattering a deadly symphony of expansion. Meanwhile, Wu-Tang's oriental mob has been operating with the precision of a well-oiled machine, striking fear into the hearts of rival bosses from the Bowery to the Battery.

"It's like watching two hurricanes collide," whispered one terrified speakeasy owner who declined to give his name. "These birds ain't fooling around - they're redrawing the map of this burg with bullets and brass knuckles."

Not to be outdone, the mysterious Vacation Steve - rumored to run his operations from a luxury yacht anchored in the harbor - snatched up 55 choice territories while supposedly "taking it easy." His lieutenant, Vacation Albert, proved that crime truly doesn't take a holiday by claiming 39 turfs of his own.

SPECIAL BULLETIN: IRON FIST STRIKES SEVENTEEN TIMES!

Wu-Tang's reign of terror reached new heights this week as his gang stormed an unprecedented SEVENTEEN enemy headquarters! The carnage began Tuesday when his boys blasted their way into SPEROD's stronghold, and continued through Thursday with brutal raids on Aharhibaba and Muna's operations. Meanwhile, Sir Caprice's crew knocked down THIRTEEN HQ doors of their own, proving that when it comes to hostile takeovers, these two kingpins wrote the book!

FALLEN EMPERORS AND DETHRONED KINGS

But for every rise, there must be a fall in this unforgiving concrete jungle. StreetKing, once the undisputed monarch of the midnight rackets, found his crown slipping as he hemorrhaged 26 territories to hungry rivals. The enigmatic Ustaz Ice saw his frozen empire begin to thaw, losing 20 prime turfs in what insiders are calling "the beginning of the end."

Perhaps most shocking of all was the precipitous decline of the FIFTH BLOOD EMPEROR, whose crimson reign lost 19 territories as younger, hungrier wolves circled his weakening pack.

FRESH BLOOD ON THE STREETS

Six new faces emerged from the shadows this week, each staking their claim to a single piece of Vendetta City's treacherous pie. Notable among the newcomers is Flash Gordon - whether he's the real space-faring hero or just another punk with delusions of grandeur remains to be seen. John James Rambo also made his bloody debut, promising that his war is far from over.

Meanwhile, a staggering 49 small-time operators simply vanished into the night - some say they're sleeping with the fishes, others whisper they've fled to Chicago with whatever cash they could carry.

POLICE BLOTTER

• Officers responded to reports of suspicious activity at Muldoon's Delicatessen, only to discover it was just the owner trying to make an honest living - charges pending.

• A horse-drawn carriage was found abandoned on Fifth Avenue filled with nothing but empty gin bottles and a note reading "The good times are killing me" - investigation ongoing.

• Local resident complained about "too much peace and quiet" in his neighborhood - police advised him to move closer to Wu-Tang's territory if he wanted more excitement.
